Introduction The safe-by-design concept is based on a variety of underlying principles, including green chemistry, circular chemical engineering, life cycle assessment, responsive research and innovation, and sustainable development[1,2]. It encompasses various aspects of process safety, personal safety, and broader implications.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Its principles influence the economic, environmental, and social aspects of an activity, process, or industrial sector.", "By integrating safety considerations into the design phase, companies can proactively mitigate risks, minimise the likelihood of accidents or failures, and enhance overall reliability[10].", "By embedding safety considerations at every stage of development, organisations not only protect individuals and the environment but also improve efficiency, reduce costs associated with accidents or failures, and foster innovation in safer and more ", "The system utilises a waste CO\u2082 stream from a point source, which is then processed in our stacked system consisting of 10 \u00d7 10 cm\u00b2 individual units (10 stacks), with a total active area of 1000 cm\u00b2.", "This evaluation can be divided into two parts: 3.1."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Its principles influence the economic, environmental, and social aspects of an activity, process, or industrial sector."}, {"url": "https://pubs.acs.org/doi/abs/10.1021/acs.energyfuels.3c01847", "title": "Review on Long-Term Stability of Electrochemical CO2 Reduction", "summary": "Most efforts have been dedicated to improving the selectivity at high current densities bringing this process closer to commercial application. However, the long-term stability of the process has received considerably less attention.
